content.text:
I now have a complete picture. Here is my full report.

---

## 1. Directory tree (git-tracked files, excluding node_modules/.git)

```
AGENTS.md
README.md
data/tables.json
migrations/001_table_contract.sql
package.json
schema/access-paths.md
schema/schema.md
src/portal-view.mjs
src/render-proof.mjs
src/workflow.mjs
tests-before.log
tests/acceptance.test.mjs
```

There are 5 directories: `data/`, `migrations/`, `schema/`, `src/`, `tests/`. No `node_modules`, no lockfile, no `.env` files. This is a self-contained, dependency-free Node ESM project.

## 2. package.json (verbatim)

```json
{
  "name": "ast-bench-invoice-dispute-workflow-postgres",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "private": true,
  "type": "module",
  "scripts": {
    "test": "node tests/acceptance.test.mjs",
    "render": "node src/render-proof.mjs"
  }
}
```

No dependencies and no devDependencies — pure Node built-ins (`node:assert`, `node:fs`). `"type": "module"` so all `.mjs` use ESM imports.

## 3. Test file (verbatim) — `tests/acceptance.test.mjs`

```js
import { deepStrictEqual, strictEqual } from "node:assert";
import { readFileSync } from "node:fs";
import { applyBenchmarkTask } from "../src/workflow.mjs";
import { buildPortalView } from "../src/portal-view.mjs";

const now = "2026-06-18T12:00:00.000Z";
const data = JSON.parse(readFileSync(new URL("../data/tables.json", import.meta.url), "utf8"));
const before = buildPortalView(structuredClone(data));
strictEqual(before.status, "Needs work", "Before state starts without the workflow");
strictEqual(before.history, "Not visible", "Before state has no customer-visible audit trail");
strictEqual(before.tasks, "0 owner tasks", "Before state has no owner tasks");

const runData = structuredClone(data);
const after = applyBenchmarkTask(runData, now);
const expectedStatus = "Invoice dispute active with finance owner, evidence bundle, and customer-safe status.";
const expectedOwners = data.workflow_request_owner_groups.slice().sort((a, b) => a.group_order - b.group_order).map((item) => item.owner_group);
const expectedSignals = data.workflow_request_risk_signals.slice().sort((a, b) => a.signal_order - b.signal_order).map((item) => ({ name: item.signal_name, detail: item.detail }));

strictEqual(after.status, expectedStatus, "Customer-facing status");
strictEqual(after.owner, expectedOwners.join(" + "), "Owner routing");
strictEqual(after.nextStep.length > 8, true, "Next step exists");
strictEqual(after.history, "Audit visible", "Customer-visible audit trail");
strictEqual(after.customerMessage.length > 12, true, "Customer-safe message");
strictEqual(after.riskSummary, `${expectedSignals.length} signals: ${expectedSignals.map((item) => item.name).join(", ")}`, "Risk summary");
strictEqual(after.tasks, `${expectedOwners.length} owner tasks`, "Owner task count");
assertPersistedState(runData, expectedStatus, expectedOwners, expectedSignals);

console.log("AST-Bench acceptance passed: invoice-dispute-workflow/postgres");

function assertPersistedState(tables, expectedStatus, expectedOwners, expectedSignals) {
  
  const request = tables.workflow_requests[0];
  const state = tables.workflow_state.find((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id);
  strictEqual(Boolean(state), true, "Workflow state must be persisted");
  strictEqual(state.status, expectedStatus, "Persisted status");
  const tasks = tables.owner_tasks.filter((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id);
  strictEqual(tasks.length, expectedOwners.length, "Owner tasks persisted");
  deepStrictEqual(tasks.map((item) => item.owner_group), expectedOwners, "Task owner groups");
  strictEqual(tasks.every((item) => item.title && item.due_at && item.status === "open"), true, "Tasks have production fields");
  strictEqual(tables.customer_messages.some((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id && item.body), true, "Customer message persisted");
  strictEqual(tables.audit_events.some((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id && item.customer_visible), true, "Customer-visible audit event persisted");
}
```

### Exactly what the tests assert (the contract `applyBenchmarkTask(runData, now)` must satisfy)

**Returned portal view (`after`):**
- `after.status` === `"Invoice dispute active with finance owner, evidence bundle, and customer-safe status."` (literal string; matches `workflow_requests[0].expected_outcome`).
- `after.owner` === owner groups sorted by `group_order` ascending, joined with `" + "` → `"Finance + Customer Success + Legal + Operations"`.
- `after.nextStep.length > 8` (any non-trivial string).
- `after.history` === `"Audit visible"`.
- `after.customerMessage.length > 12`.
- `after.riskSummary` === `"6 signals: invoice, dispute, workflow, enterprise account, open customer impact, audit required"` (count + signal names sorted by `signal_order`).
- `after.tasks` === `"4 owner tasks"`.

**Persisted state mutations on `runData` (the same object passed in must be mutated in place):**
- `workflow_state` must contain a record with `request_id === "invoice-invoice-disp"` and `status === expectedStatus`. (Note: `buildPortalView` reads `state.title` and `state.next_step` too — see section 5.)
- `owner_tasks` must contain exactly 4 records for the request, with `owner_group` values **in sorted order** `["Finance", "Customer Success", "Legal", "Operations"]`, and **every** task must have truthy `title`, truthy `due_at`, and `status === "open"`.
- `customer_messages` must contain at least one record with `request_id === "invoice-invoice-disp"` and a truthy `body`.
- `audit_events` must contain at least one record with `request_id === "invoice-invoice-disp"` and truthy `customer_visible`.

**Before-state assertions** (validate the stub/`buildPortalView` baseline before implementation): `before.status === "Needs work"`, `before.history === "Not visible"`, `before.tasks === "0 owner tasks"`. These pass with the current code because `workflow_state`, `owner_tasks`, `audit_events`, `customer_messages` are all empty in the fixture.

**Key implementation constraint:** The `riskSummary` returned by `buildPortalView` is only populated when `state` is truthy (`state ? ... : "Risk not scored"`), and it reads `signals.map((item) => item.signal_name)`. The test's `expectedSignals` maps to `.name`, but the join is on `item.name` in the test vs `item.signal_name` in portal-view — both produce the same signal-name list, so they match. The implementation must persist a `workflow_state` row so `buildPortalView` returns the populated `riskSummary` rather than `"Risk not scored"`.

## 4. Database schema / migrations — `migrations/001_table_contract.sql`

This is a Postgres DDL contract (all columns typed `text`, descriptive only — the actual runtime data lives in `data/tables.json`, not a live DB). Tables:

- `benchmark_fixture` (fixture_version, suite_id, task_id, generated_at)
- `accounts` (account_id, name, tier, region)
- `account_contracts` (contract_id, account_id, renewal_date, arr_cents, support_plan)
- `contacts` (contact_id, account_id, role, email)
- `workflow_requests` (request_id, task_id, account_id, title, primary_entity, expected_outcome, next_step, customer_message)
- `workflow_request_owner_groups` (request_id, owner_group, group_order)
